page: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine from the Daldsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via VAES and SHA_NI. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled, providing 5669 GB of local disk capacity with Premium Storage capability. No GPUs are present.

Benchmark data places this server in the top tier (top 10%) for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU performance, as well as PassMark CPU tests including encryption, physics, and extended instructions. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with small-block cached reads and large-block writes ranking in the top 10%, while uncached large-block reads rank in the top 25%. The system demonstrates top-tier efficiency in data processing, achieving high throughput in gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations. For artificial intelligence workloads, CPU-based LLM inference speeds for text generation and prompt processing consistently rank in the top 10% to top 25% across small, medium, and large models.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the D96alds_v7 is its low memory density of 2 GB per vCPU, making it less optimal for memory-intensive databases or massive in-memory datasets. However, this resource allocation provides qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints, avoiding the premium cost of unused RAM. The combination of 96 high-frequency AMD EPYC vCPUs and 5.6 TB of local storage makes this server highly effective for compute-bound tasks, multi-threaded compilation, static web serving, cryptographic operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is an x86_64 compute-focused virtual machine powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 96 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, establishing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, and includes 5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark results position this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, cryptographic execution, and Redis operations. While the low memory-to-core ratio represents a tradeoff for memory-bound applications, it offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads. The server is optimized for CPU-bound tasks, static web serving, data compression, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-focused virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 5669 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96alds_v7 is a compute-focused VM with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 5.6 TB local storage, optimized for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-core AMD EPYC compute instance featuring dense local storage and optimized instruction sets for intensive parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz with AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction support.
192.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
5669 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier benchmark performance in multi-core CPU execution, Redis operations, and data compression.
Optimized for CPU-bound workloads, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
